info.title: CENEMS IoT API
info.description: API for managing IoT devices, sites, and energy readings

get.description: Root endpoint.
Health check endpoint.
Get all organizations with pagination.
Get organization by ID.

patch.description: Partial update of organization settings (admin/owner only).

get.description: List all members of an organization. Returns 404 if org_id != X-Org-Id.

schema.title: Response Get Org Members Organizations  Org Id  Members Get

post.description: Invite a user to the organization (admin/owner only).

delete.description: Remove a member from the organization (admin/owner only).

get.description: Get all users with pagination.
Get user by ID.
Return current user profile, org role, and preferences.
Return the current user's preferences.

patch.description: Upsert (create or update) the current user's preferences.

get.description: Get all sites with pagination.
Get site by ID.

delete.description: Delete a site and cascade delete its devices. Readings are preserved.

get.description:
Return the latest reading per power-flow metric for a site.

Intended for polling every 10–15 seconds to drive the power-flow
display in the new UI.  Only metrics listed in METRIC_KEY_MAP are
included; any signal_id not in that set is ignored.

Uses a single DISTINCT ON query (PostgreSQL) — never N+1.
signal_readings.signal_id stores the metric name (e.g. GRID_IMPORT).
The numeric value is stored in the JSONB data column as data->>'value'.

Response includes:
- power_flow: dict of power-flow keys to latest float values
- signal_ages: dict of metric name to age in seconds (only populated
  metrics are included)
- as_of: ISO-8601 timestamp of the newest reading among the returned rows

schema.title: Response Get Site Current State Sites  Site Id  Current State Get

get.description:
Return the latest reading per signal_id for all signals at a site.

Intended for polling every 10–15 seconds to populate the device-block
grid in the new UI.

Uses a single DISTINCT ON query (PostgreSQL) — never N+1.
The numeric value is stored in the JSONB data column as data->>'value'.
Each row includes age_seconds computed from the reading timestamp to now.

Query param:
- limit: max signals to return (default 50, max 200)

schema.title: Response Get Site Recent Signals Sites  Site Id  Recent Signals Get

get.description:
Return commissioning readiness checklist and score for a site.

Computes six weighted checks and returns the overall readiness score
(0-100), whether the site is ready (score >= 80), the individual check
items, and a list of blocking issues for any failed required checks.

schema.title: Response Get Commissioning Status Sites  Site Id  Commissioning Status Get

post.description:
Commission a site atomically.

Checks commissioning readiness. If the site is not ready, returns 422
with the list of blocking issues.

If ready, in a single transaction:
- Sets site.commissioned_at = now()
- Sets site.creation_step = 100 (complete)
- Sets all devices to commissioning_status = "commissioned"

schema.title: Response Commission Site Sites  Site Id  Commission Post

get.description:
Return the wizard creation progress for a site.

Returns creation_step (0-100), the set of completed step milestones,
and the label for the current step.

schema.title: Response Get Creation Progress Sites  Site Id  Creation Progress Get

patch.description:
Update the wizard creation step for a site.

Accepts a step value between 0 and 100.

schema.title: Response Update Creation Step Sites  Site Id  Creation Step Patch

post.description:
Finalize a site — set creation_step to 100 to mark setup as complete.

Does NOT perform commissioning validation; use POST /commission for that.

schema.title: Response Finalize Site Sites  Site Id  Finalize Post

get.description:
Return the full 4-component health breakdown for a site.

Components: connectivity (30%), freshness (35%), quality (20%), device_health (15%).
Scores 0-100; >= 75 healthy, 50-74 warning, < 50 critical.

schema.title: Response Get Site Health Breakdown Sites  Site Id  Health Get

get.description: Return paginated SiteHealthEvent records for a site (most recent first).

schema.title: Response Get Site Health History Sites  Site Id  Health History Get

patch.description:
Upsert per-org health thresholds.

Merges provided fields with existing row (or defaults). Returns updated config.
site_id is verified for org ownership but thresholds are org-scoped.

schema.title: Response Update Health Thresholds Sites  Site Id  Health Thresholds Patch

get.description: Get all devices with optional filters.
Get device by ID.

patch.description: Update a device (partial update). Note: serialNumber is immutable.

delete.description: Delete a device. Readings are preserved.

post.description:
Create multiple devices in a single request.

Returns HTTP 207 Multi-Status with two lists:
- created: successfully inserted device objects
- failed: items that could not be inserted, with index and error message

Valid items are inserted even when some items fail (partial success).
Each item is attempted inside a savepoint so failures do not roll back
previously inserted items.

schema.title: Response Bulk Create Devices Devices Bulk Create Post

post.description:
Deep-copy a device.

Creates a new device with the same device_type_id and site_id, with
name suffixed " (copy)". Returns 201 with the new device data.

schema.title: Response Clone Device Devices  Device Id  Clone Post

get.description:
Return the device configuration JSONB dict.

Returns an empty dict if no configuration has been set.
Returns 404 if the device does not exist or belongs to a different org.

schema.title: Response Get Device Configuration Devices  Device Id  Configuration Get

patch.description:
Merge-patch the device configuration JSONB.

Validates the merged result against device_type.configuration_schema if
one exists.  Performs a shallow merge (not a deep replace) so that callers
can update individual keys without supplying the full config.

Returns the updated configuration dict.

schema.title: Response Patch Device Configuration Devices  Device Id  Configuration Patch

get.description: Get readings with optional filters.
Get reading by ID.
Get overview statistics.
Get device type distribution.
Get all API keys.

schema.title: Response Get Api Keys Api Keys Get

post.description: Create a new API key (admin/owner only).

delete.description:
Soft-delete an API key by setting revoked_at and revoked_by.

Returns 404 if the key is not found or belongs to a different org.

patch.description:
Update API key name and/or description.

Returns 404 if the key is not found or belongs to a different org.

post.description:
Generate a CSV report of readings for specified devices and date range.

The report will:
- Include all readings for the specified devices within the date range
- Flatten nested JSON data fields using double underscore (__) separator
- Handle variable JSON schemas across different readings
- Leave fields blank for missing values

List Handling:
- If unpackLists=false (default): Lists are converted to JSON strings
- If unpackLists=true: Lists are unpacked with index-based keys (e.g., items__0__name, items__1__name)

Constraints:
- Start date must be within 30 days from today
- End date must be after start date
- At least one device ID must be provided
Generate a CSV report of signal readings for specified devices, templates, blocks, and signals.

get.description: Get signal readings with optional filters.

schema.title: Response Get Signal Readings Signal Readings Get

get.description: Get signal reading by ID.
List all dashboards for the current organization.
Get a dashboard by ID with its full configuration.

Get available filter options for dashboard panels.

Returns lists of:
- Available signals
- Available metric windows
- Available metric types
- Available sites
- Available devices
- Available templates
- Available blocks

schema.title: Response Get Filter Options Dashboards Options Filters Get

patch.description:
Update the sharing scope and shared user list for a dashboard.

Requires admin or owner role. Returns 404 for unknown or cross-tenant dashboards.

post.description:
Deep-copy a dashboard (config including widgets) into a new dashboard
owned by the requesting user.

Returns 404 for unknown or cross-tenant dashboards.

get.description:
List all commands for the current organization.
Supports filtering by device_id, site_id, status, and time range.

post.description: Create a new command to send to a device.

get.description: Get a command by ID.

put.description: Update a command. Only PENDING commands can be updated.

delete.description:
Delete a command.
If the command is in progress, it will be cancelled instead.

patch.description:
Update the status of a command.
Used by device agents or the system to report command execution results.

get.description:
Query signal aggregations with filters.

Time range defaults to last 24 hours if not specified.

parameters.name: metric_window

schema.description: Filter by metric window (e.g., '1 hour', '1 day')
schema.title: Metric Window

parameters.description: Filter by metric window (e.g., '1 hour', '1 day')

parameters.name: from

schema.description: Start time (relative like '-24h' or absolute ISO date)
schema.title: From

parameters.description: Start time (relative like '-24h' or absolute ISO date)

get.description:
Get the latest aggregation value for each signal.

This is a convenience endpoint for getting current values without
specifying a time range.
Get available metric types and windows for UI dropdowns.

get.summary: Get active forecast for a site
get.description:
Get the latest active forecast for a site via the live pointer.

Resolves the ``ForecastLivePointer`` for the given site and org, then
loads the referenced ``ForecastRun`` and all of its ``ForecastPoint``
rows ordered by timestamp.

Returns a combined response with run metadata and the ordered list of
forecast points.  Fields that do not yet exist on the model are returned
as ``null`` so the schema remains forward-compatible.

Args:
    site_id: Site identifier from the URL path.
    x_org_id: Tenant identifier extracted from the ``X-Org-Id`` header.
    db: Injected SQLAlchemy session.

Returns:
    Dict containing forecast metadata and ``points`` list.

Raises:
    HTTPException 404: When no live pointer exists for the site/org pair,
        or when the referenced forecast run no longer exists.

schema.title: Response Get Active Forecast Forecasting Sites  Site Id  Active Get

get.summary: List forecast run history for a site
get.description:
Return a paginated list of forecast runs for a site.

All runs are filtered by both ``site_id`` and the ``org_id`` derived from
the ``X-Org-Id`` header so cross-tenant data is never exposed.  Runs are
returned newest-first (``generated_at`` descending).

Args:
    site_id: Site identifier from the URL path.
    limit: Maximum number of runs to return (default 50, max 200).
    offset: Number of runs to skip for pagination (default 0).
    x_org_id: Tenant identifier extracted from the ``X-Org-Id`` header.
    db: Injected SQLAlchemy session.

Returns:
    Dict with ``total`` count and ``runs`` list.

schema.title: Response List Forecast History Forecasting Sites  Site Id  History Get

get.summary: Get all data points for a specific forecast run
get.description:
Return all forecast data points for the given forecast run.

The run is verified to belong to the requesting org before any data is
returned.  If the run does not exist or belongs to a different org a 404
is returned to avoid leaking resource existence.

Args:
    forecast_id: UUID of the forecast run from the URL path.
    x_org_id: Tenant identifier extracted from the ``X-Org-Id`` header.
    db: Injected SQLAlchemy session.

Returns:
    Dict with ``forecast_id``, ``site_id``, and ``points`` list ordered
    by ``slot_index``.

Raises:
    HTTPException 404: When the forecast run does not exist or belongs
        to a different org.
    HTTPException 422: When ``forecast_id`` is not a valid UUID.

schema.title: Response Get Forecast Points Forecasting Forecasts  Forecast Id  Points Get

get.summary: Get accuracy metrics for a completed forecast run
get.description:
Return accuracy metrics for a completed forecast run.

Scoped to the requesting org — returns 404 for cross-tenant or
non-existent runs.

Args:
    forecast_id: UUID of the forecast run.
    x_org_id: Tenant identifier from the ``X-Org-Id`` header.
    db: Injected SQLAlchemy session.

Returns:
    Dict with accuracy KPIs: mae_kw, rmse_kw, mape_percent,
    coverage_percent, confidence_score, model_name, window_start,
    window_end.

Raises:
    HTTPException 404: Run not found or belongs to a different org.
    HTTPException 422: forecast_id is not a valid UUID.

schema.title: Response Get Forecast Accuracy Forecasting Forecasts  Forecast Id  Accuracy Get

get.summary: Time-aligned forecast vs actual comparison for charting
get.description:
Return time-aligned predicted vs actual kW values for charting.

Joins ForecastPoint rows through their parent ForecastRun to verify
org ownership — no cross-tenant data is returned.  Only points that
have ``actual_value_kw`` populated are included.

Query params:
    start_ts: ISO datetime for the start of the range.
    end_ts: ISO datetime for the end of the range.

Returns:
    Dict with ``site_id`` and ``points`` list.

schema.title: Response Get Forecast Vs Actuals Forecasting Sites  Site Id  Forecast Vs Actuals Get

get.summary: Portfolio KPI rollups for an org or portfolio scope
get.description:
Return portfolio-level KPI rollups for the given scope and date range.

``scope`` must be ``"org"`` or a portfolio identifier string.  All
ForecastRun rows within the date window and matching the org are
aggregated.

Returns:
    Dict with aggregated KPIs: avg_coverage_percent, avg_mae_kw,
    total_forecast_runs, sites_with_forecast, sites_without_forecast,
    estimated_savings_kwh.

schema.title: Response Get Forecast Rollups Forecasting Rollups  Scope  Get

get.summary: Last run info and next scheduled time for a site
get.description:
Return last run info and next scheduled time for a site.

``next_scheduled_at`` is computed as ``last_run.generated_at + 24 hours``.
Returns 404 when no runs exist for the site in the given org.

Returns:
    Dict with ``site_id``, ``last_run`` metadata, and
    ``next_scheduled_at`` ISO timestamp.

schema.title: Response Get Site Run Status Forecasting Sites  Site Id  Run Status Get

get.summary: Model assumptions for the active forecast of a site
get.description:
Return model assumptions read from the most recent ForecastRun's metadata.

Resolves the latest run for the site + org and returns model-level
fields.  The ``assumptions`` dict contains static defaults; if the
ForecastRun model later grows a JSONB assumptions column those values
will be preferred.

Returns:
    Dict with ``site_id``, ``model_name``, ``last_updated``, and
    ``assumptions`` dict.

Raises:
    HTTPException 404: No runs found for the site in the requesting org.

schema.title: Response Get Site Assumptions Forecasting Sites  Site Id  Assumptions Get

get.description: List all available templates.

Get a template with its full nested structure (blocks with their signals).

This endpoint is useful for understanding the complete hierarchy of a template.
List all blocks, optionally filtered by template.
Get a block with its signals.
List all signals, optionally filtered by block.
Get a signal by ID.

List device-signal mappings with optional filtering.

Use this to see which signals are mapped to which devices,
and what JSON paths are used to extract values.

post.description:
Create a new device-signal mapping.

This defines how a signal value is extracted from device data
using the specified JSON path.

get.description: Get a device-signal mapping by ID.

put.description:
Update a device-signal mapping.

You can update the path, block_id, or template_id.
To change device_id or signal_id, delete and recreate the mapping.

schema.title: Response List Device Types Device Types Get

get.description:
Return the JSON Schema for the device type's configuration field.

Returns an empty object if no schema has been defined.
Returns 404 if the device type does not exist.

Device types are not org-scoped (they are global/shared), so no
X-Org-Id header is required.

schema.title: Response Get Device Type Configuration Schema Device Types  Device Type Id  Configuration Schema Get

get.description:
Return the org-wide overview aggregate for the new-UI dashboard.

Combines org health score, open attention item counts, and per-module
status rollup. All data is scoped to the org represented by X-Org-Id.

Return a paginated list of attention items for the org.

Filters: severity (comma-separated), module, resolved.
Results are ordered by severity priority, then exposure, then recency.
Each item includes site_name (joined from sites table) and indicator_color.

schema.title: Response List Attention Items Overview Attention Items Get

post.description:
Manually resolve an attention item.

Sets resolved_at to now and auto_resolved to False.
Returns 404 if the item does not exist or belongs to a different org.

schema.title: Response Resolve Attention Item Overview Attention Items  Item Id  Resolve Post

get.description:
Return org-wide portfolio health summary.

Aggregates health_state / health_score from the sites table for all sites
in the org. Returns per-state counts, average score, and a flat site list.

schema.title: Response Get Portfolio Health Overview Health Get

get.description:
Return health breakdown for a single site.

Returns the site's health_state, health_score, and open attention item counts.
Returns 404 if the site does not exist or belongs to a different org.

schema.title: Response Get Site Health Overview Site Health  Site Id  Get

get.description:
Return sites sorted by health_score ASC (worst first) for the operations dashboard.

Includes site name, health_score, health_state, and attention_item_count
(count of open attention items joined from attention_items table).

schema.title: Response Get Operations Sites Operations Sites Get
